questions.question: What is a class in C++?
questions.explanation: A class is a user-defined blueprint or prototype from which objects are created.

questions.question: Which access specifier makes members accessible only within the class?
questions.explanation: Private members are accessible only within the class itself.

questions.question: What is inheritance in C++?
options: A class acquiring properties of another class
questions.explanation: Inheritance allows a class to inherit properties and methods from another class.

questions.question: What is polymorphism?
questions.explanation: Polymorphism means 'many forms' and allows objects to be treated as instances of their parent class.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to inherit a class in C++?
questions.explanation: In C++, the colon (:) is used to specify inheritance.
